name: Wine and Fish Route: Lekeitio Loop
name_original: Orilla del río Lea – circular desde Lequeitio por el Faro de Santa Catalina
description: This difficult 19.6-mile touring cycling route from Lekeitio offers coastal views and a visit to the Santa Catalina Lighthouse.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Lekeitio features a coastal landscape with varied terrain, including significant elevation changes and scenic views of the Bay of Biscay. The region is characterized by its proximity to the sea, offering routes that combine seaside stretches with inland climbs through hills and valleys. This area provides a mix of challenging ascents and rewarding vistas for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Lekeitio The most popular touring cycling route is Wine and Fish Route: Lekeitio Loop, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic coastal views and inland paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Lekeitio – Lekeitio loop from Lekeitio, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and panoramic views of the town and coastline. Local touring cyclists also love the Lekeitio – View of Lekeitio loop from Mendexa, a 17.0 miles (27.4 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and offering elevated perspectives, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Touring cycling around Lekeitio is defined by coastal paths, rolling hills, and scenic viewpoints overlooking the sea. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier coastal rides to more advanced cycling routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Lekeitio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 1,200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lekeitio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Lekeitio?
A: There are nearly 60 touring cycling routes around Lekeitio, offering a wide range of options for exploring the coastal landscape and inland areas.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes in Lekeitio for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes feature significant elevation changes, there are a few easier options. Lekeitio offers 2 easy routes and 10 moderate routes suitable for those looking for less strenuous rides or family outings. These routes often stick closer to the coast or follow gentler inland paths.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the touring cycling routes around Lekeitio?
A: The routes around Lekeitio are known for their scenic coastal views of the Bay of Biscay, combined with inland paths through rolling hills and valleys. Many routes offer panoramic vistas of the town and coastline, such as those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38191044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lekeitio – Lekeitio loop from Lekeitio&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Lekeitio area?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Lekeitio are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068528898/wine-and-fish-route-lekeitio-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wine and Fish Route: Lekeitio Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers both coastal and inland scenery.

Q: What are some interesting places to explore or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes, you can discover various natural and historical attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5540278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Ea&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/426218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of the Assumption of Our Lady of Lekeitio&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoying the views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7987400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Basque Coast Cliffs&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4977779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coastal Road Ondarroa–Lekeitio&lt;/a&gt; is also a highlight in itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lekeitio?
A: Lekeitio, being a coastal region, generally experiences mild winters and pleasant springs and autumns. The shoulder seasons (spring and autumn) often provide ideal cycling conditions with comfortable temperatures and less crowded routes, allowing you to fully enjoy the coastal and inland scenery.

Q: Are there challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Lekeitio is known for its varied terrain, including significant elevation changes. Out of nearly 60 routes, 47 are graded as difficult, offering substantial climbs and rewarding descents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38187112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lekeitio – Laga Beach loop from Mendexa&lt;/a&gt; provide a considerable challenge with over 1700 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Lekeitio?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lekeitio are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.1 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the combination of scenic coastal views, challenging climbs, and the diverse terrain that makes each ride unique.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes in Lekeitio?
A: Lekeitio is connected by local bus services that can facilitate access to the town and potentially some starting points for routes. However, specific information on bike transport on public buses should be checked with local operators, as policies can vary.

Q: Are there any routes that take me past historical sites or natural monuments?
A: Yes, several routes offer opportunities to see historical sites and natural monuments. For instance, you might pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5540833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrola Oppidum&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4056489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Miguel de Arretxinaga&lt;/a&gt;. The region's diverse landscape means you're often cycling through areas with rich history and natural beauty.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a cycling route in Lekeitio?
A: Lekeitio, as a popular coastal town, offers various public parking areas. It's advisable to look for designated parking zones within or on the outskirts of the town, especially near the starting points of popular routes, though availability can vary, particularly during peak season.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the touring cycling routes?
A: Given the routes often pass through or near towns and villages, you will likely find opportunities for refreshments. Lekeitio itself has numerous cafes and restaurants, and smaller establishments may be found in other communities along the way, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40327958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lekeitio – View of Lekeitio loop from Mendexa&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Good views and the cafe is nice for a coffee/beer/snack with a view

Lekeitio is an emblem for Bizkaia and the Basque Country in general. A town with a fishing tradition with a beautiful old town. Its fishermen's houses with colorful balconies and port will captivate you.

The Basilica of the Assumption dates back to the 15th century and is an important Late Gothic construction that has been declared a National Monument. Striking are its appearance, especially the western façade, and its magnificent sixteenth-century flamenco gothic retable considered to be the best of Vizcaya, as well as a 12th-century Marian sculpture Antiguako Ama (in Euskara), in its own Baroque altarpiece , Source and further information: https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lequeitio and https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bas%C3%ADlica_de_la_Asunci%C3%B3n_de_Nuestra_Se%C3%B1ora_(Lequeitio)
Recreational area next to the river Lea as it passes through the town of Aulesti. The area has benches and tables for a picnic, as well as a park for the little ones.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 31.6 km (19.6 miles) long and generally takes around 2 hours and 33 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. Keep in mind that this duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery or visit attractions like the Santa Catalina Lighthouse.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like for this trail?
faqs.answer: The route is categorized as difficult for cycling, demanding a high level of fitness. While much of it features paved surfaces, be prepared for some unpaved segments that can be challenging and may require you to dismount and push your bike. The significant elevation gain of approximately 460 meters also contributes to its demanding nature.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque coastal views and passes through the charming fishing village of Lekeitio. A major highlight is the Santa Catalina Lighthouse, the only visitable lighthouse in the Basque Country, which houses an interpretive center. You'll also pass by the impressive Basilica of the Assumption of Our Lady of Lekeitio and the Río Lea Recreation Area.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating, significant elevation gain, and potential for unpaved sections, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires a high level of fitness and cycling experience. For a more relaxed experience, consider exploring shorter, flatter sections around Lekeitio.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Lekeitio?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Lekeitio, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas in Lekeitio, especially near the harbor or town center, which provide convenient access to the starting point of the route. Specific trailhead parking is not designated, but town parking serves the purpose.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is usually milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the potentially wetter, colder conditions of winter. This allows for comfortable enjoyment of the coastal views.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact cycling route are not explicitly stated, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash if you bring them. Given that it's a cycling route, managing a dog while riding can be challenging, especially on busier sections or unpaved terrain. Always be mindful of other cyclists and local signage regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the River Lea Bank circular route. However, if you plan to visit the interpretive center inside the Santa Catalina Lighthouse, there may be a small admission fee. Check their official website or local information for current details.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers a significant portion (80%) of the "Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea (Ziortza-Lekeitio)" and about 22% of the "Bizkaiko bira". You'll also find sections that overlap with the "Lea ibilbidea" at various points, offering opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: Expect stunning panoramic views, especially along the coastal sections. You'll be treated to picturesque vistas of the Basque coast and the expansive Bay of Biscay. The route also offers charming views of the fishing village of Lekeitio, nestled between mountains and the sea, and the scenic surroundings of the River Lea.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Lekeitio, a town with various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms. Along the route itself, facilities are more limited, though you will pass the Río Lea Recreation Area which may offer some basic facilities. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks, especially for the more remote sections.

waypoints.name: Basilica of the Assumption of Our Lady of Lekeitio
